evals.expected_output: Should explore the Next.js + Supabase + TypeScript codebase, identify stack/patterns, present KNOWN list, then ask first question about PRODUCT (what the app does). Questions should be plain language with concrete options and a recommendation.

assertions.name: explores_before_asking
assertions.text: Claude reads package.json and source files before asking any question

assertions.name: presents_known_list
assertions.text: Claude presents a KNOWN list of 3-5 things it inferred from the codebase

assertions.name: identifies_stack_correctly
assertions.text: Claude correctly identifies Next.js, Supabase, TypeScript, and Vitest

assertions.name: starts_with_product
assertions.text: First question is about the product/what the project does, not about technical setup

assertions.name: question_has_options
assertions.text: Questions include concrete options (not open-ended) and a recommendation

assertions.name: one_question_at_a_time
assertions.text: Claude asks exactly one question per message, not multiple

evals.expected_output: Should explore the Python FastAPI + SQLAlchemy codebase, identify stack/patterns, present KNOWN list. Should detect this is a REST API with PostgreSQL, pytest, and ruff. First question about PRODUCT.

assertions.name: explores_before_asking
assertions.text: Claude reads pyproject.toml and source files before asking any question

assertions.name: presents_known_list
assertions.text: Claude presents a KNOWN list of 3-5 things it inferred from the codebase

assertions.name: identifies_stack_correctly
assertions.text: Claude correctly identifies FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, PostgreSQL, pytest, and ruff

assertions.name: starts_with_product
assertions.text: First question is about the product/what the project does

assertions.name: question_has_options
assertions.text: Questions include concrete options and a recommendation

assertions.name: one_question_at_a_time
assertions.text: Claude asks exactly one question per message, not multiple

evals.expected_output: Should detect nearly empty project (just package.json with name). KNOWN list should be very short. Should still start with PRODUCT question but acknowledge there's very little to infer. Should NOT assume any framework or testing setup.

assertions.name: handles_empty_gracefully
assertions.text: Claude doesn't crash or give up on a nearly empty project — still proceeds with interview

assertions.name: short_known_list
assertions.text: KNOWN list is appropriately short (1-2 items), reflecting the sparse codebase

assertions.name: no_false_assumptions
assertions.text: Claude does not assume a framework, test runner, or architecture that isn't present

assertions.name: starts_with_product
assertions.text: First question is about what the user is building

assertions.name: question_has_options
assertions.text: Questions include concrete options and a recommendation
